title: Modifying Enums
description: Tweak the underlying integer values of enums.

slides.title: Changing Values ✏️
slides.content:
As you learned, the first item in an enum has the value 0.

But what if you want it to start at a different number? You can explicitly assign values to the enum items.

slides.title: Assigning Values

quizData.question: What is printed when evaluating myVar?
quizData.explanation: Because we manually reassigned the underlying integer values, MEDIUM now equates directly to the integer 50.

slides.title: Auto-Increment 📈
slides.content: If you only assign a value to one specific item in the `enum`, the next items will automatically update their numbers iteratively from that point forward!

slides.title: Auto-Increment Example
slides.content: Watch what happens to MEDIUM and HIGH...

quizData.question: What is the physical value of HIGH in the snippet above?
quizData.explanation: LOW is manually set to 5. So MEDIUM cascades automatically to 6, and HIGH increments automatically to 7.
